# Lab N — Title

**Objective.** One sentence describing what the student will produce or
demonstrate by the end of the session.

**Pre-requisites.** Concepts or chapters that must be understood first.

**Required tools.** Hardware, software, library versions.

## 1. Background

Two or three paragraphs of prose situating the lab within the broader subject.

## 2. Procedure

Numbered steps the student executes:

1. Setup — what to install or wire up.
2. Configuration — exact commands or values.
3. Execution — the experiment itself.
4. Capture — what to record (screenshot, output, measurement).

## 3. Expected output

What "working" looks like. Include screenshots or sample logs in a fenced block.

## 4. Analysis questions

Two or three open questions for the student to answer in their report — the
questions should require synthesising the procedure, not regurgitating it.

## 5. Deliverables

What gets submitted, in what format, by when. Cross-link to the course's
grading rubric if one exists.
